title: An Escaped African Serval Turned Up Under a Vancouver Porch
script: Wild cat under the porch! On June 9, a Vancouver homeowner near Prince Albert Street and East 30th Avenue called police after discovering a large spotted wildcat had taken shelter beneath their house. Officers arrived to find a full-grown African serval — a wild cat native to sub-Saharan Africa that stands over 2 feet tall and weighs up to 40 pounds. Conservation officers worked with police to safely catch the animal and return it to its owners. British Columbia only banned new exotic cat ownership in May, under the Controlled Alien Species Regulation. Any serval already in the province before that date can be kept under a free permit. Neighbors had no idea a serval lived nearby. How many undeclared exotic cats are hiding in Vancouver right now?
